steps.title: Apply both planet criteria
steps.content: Hot and warm Jupiters must each have a mass of at least 0.25 Jupiters. Hot Jupiters have orbital periods under 10 days, while warm Jupiters have orbital periods over 10 days.

steps.title: Check mass and orbit data
steps.content: Choice A wrongly says none have periods over 10 days. Choice B would make one planet warm but does not support four hot Jupiters. Choice C uses radius, which is irrelevant to the definitions. The supporting data must include all masses and the one longer orbit. The answer is D.
